description:
The wild-type SARS-CoV-2 Spike RBD bound to ACE2 (PDB ID: 6M0J) has been pre-loaded into your PyMOL session. Your task is to perform an in silico point mutation to model the Alpha variant.

1. Using PyMOL's Mutagenesis wizard (or programmatic commands), mutate residue Asn501 on the Spike RBD (Chain E) to Tyrosine (TYR).
2. Select a rotamer for the new Tyrosine that points its hydroxyl group (-OH) towards Tyr41 on the ACE2 receptor (Chain A) to optimize the interaction.
3. Save the *entire* mutated complex as a PDB file to `~/PyMOL_Data/structures/6m0j_N501Y.pdb`.
4. Measure the distance between the sidechain hydroxyl oxygen (OH atom) of the newly mutated Tyr501 (Chain E) and the sidechain hydroxyl oxygen (OH atom) of Tyr41 (Chain A).
5. Create a clear visualization focusing on the Y501-Y41 interaction, showing both residues as sticks, and render a high-quality screenshot saved to `~/PyMOL_Data/images/n501y_interaction.png`.
6. Write a structural report to `~/PyMOL_Data/n501y_report.txt` containing the measured OH-OH distance in Angstroms.
